notes: BG 2.9 is pure narration (Sanjaya describing Arjuna's 'na yotsya iti' declaration and his subsequent silence). The engineering layer correctly identifies it as scaffolding, not doctrine: 'This verse is narrative scaffolding, not engineering doctrine.' The Sanskrit's specific load-bearing element — the emphatic particle 'ha' in tūṣṇīṁ babhūva ha — is preserved in spirit by the engineering layer's recognition that 'the silence is structurally significant.' The literal claim ('I will not fight' followed by silence) is rendered without distortion in the analog ('I am not going to ship this' followed by going dark on the channel). The verse's role as the precondition for 2.11's teaching is correctly identified: 'It simply marks the moment that Arjuna's case has closed and the teaching is about to open.' The engineering layer also explicitly disclaims that the silence is itself a teaching, which honors the consensus reading that this verse 'has no doctrinal content of its own.' PASS — the narrative tag is honestly applied, no Sanskrit claim is inverted or fabricated.
